response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack software developer with strong web, cloud, and developer-tooling experience spanning 14+ years, but has no demonstrated background in SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, deep learning workload optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD systems — the core technical pillars of this Principal Performance Architect role. His low-latency microservices work at Dunnhumby and recent AI-agent tooling at re:cinq show adjacent competence but do not bridge the substantial gap to hardware-level performance engineering for AI accelerators.
strengths: 14+ years of engineering experience across varied domains, satisfying the BS + 8 years education/experience requirement
Demonstrated performance-oriented engineering: Asynchronous I/O, caching, fibers, and high-throughput microservices handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby
Recent hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq, showing awareness of modern AI tooling ecosystems
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware design experience whatsoever — the primary and non-negotiable technical requirement of the role
No background in deep neural network training or inference optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD parallel architectures
Career trajectory is firmly anchored in full-stack web and cloud software development with zero signal of transition toward hardware-software co-design

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware modeling experience
No deep learning or AI accelerator workload expertise
